summary: Simulation and evaluation infrastructure for training reliable AI agents.
editor_note: The sharp bet is that agent safety will be trained in worlds, not audited after deployment. Digital World Models, RL environments, Lynx and Glider evaluators, and a $50M Series B make it feel closer to infrastructure than another eval dashboard. The risk is whether simulated software work predicts messy enterprise workflows well enough.

product_evidence.claim: The official homepage says Digital World Models predict and simulate agent actions in digital workflows.
The products page lists a core platform for experiments, logging, comparisons, and traces alongside Lynx, Glider, LLM-as-a-Judge, and Digital World Models.
The official announcement says Patronus-DWM is evaluated across coding, dialogue, research, GUI, API, and general tool-use benchmark domains.

market_context.primary_user: AI research, agent, and platform engineering teams that need to evaluate long-horizon agent behavior before production deployment.
market_context.differentiation: Patronus is moving from post-hoc LLM evaluation into simulated digital work environments for training and testing agents.
market_context.why_now: Autonomous agents are shifting from chat responses to software, research, and tool-use workflows where failures need pre-production simulation.

risks.claim: The open question is whether simulated digital workflows predict enterprise-specific agent failures closely enough to become a deployment gate.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on the official product surface and funding-source review.
